# Documentation Discipline

## The Physics

Knowledge that exists only in someone's head is organizationally invisible.
It cannot be audited, searched, taught, or verified. When that person is
unavailable — or when an agent needs the information — undocumented knowledge
is functionally nonexistent.

This is not a bureaucratic preference. It is an operational reality: systems
and agents can only act on recorded information. The cost of documentation is
always less than the cost of rediscovery.

## Why It Matters

- **Continuity**: Documented decisions survive personnel changes.
- **Agent operability**: AI agents can only use what has been written down.
- **Auditability**: Undocumented actions cannot be reviewed or improved.
- **Redundancy**: Documentation IS the backup for institutional knowledge.

## Academic/Industry Backing

- Aviation: checklists and flight records as safety infrastructure (Gawande, "The Checklist Manifesto").
- Legal: "If it isn't documented, it didn't happen" as legal standard of care.
- Software: runbooks, ADRs (Architecture Decision Records), postmortems.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-C05 (Data Primacy) — documentation is the act of creating organizational data
- JOS-P03 (Traceability) — traceability requires documentation
- JOS-S03 (Documentation Governance) — the standard for how documentation is structured
